Comment: deploy results-api governor+BMC dual fetch, sorkor export, and BMC mock ticker

commands: sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --no-deps --build results-api'

sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && for i in $(seq 1 30); do status=$(docker inspect --format "{{if .State.Health}}{{.State.Health.Status}}{{else}}{{.State.Status}}{{end}}" election-results-api-1); echo results-api=$status; if [ "$status" = "healthy" ] || [ "$status" = "running" ]; then break; fi; sleep 2; done'
sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose exec -T results-api python -m unittest hermes.governor_results.test_sorkor_adapter hermes.governor_results.test_public_source -v'

sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose exec -T results-api python - <<"PY"
from hermes.results_api.app import effective_external_bmc_results_config, perform_monitor_fetch
bmc = effective_external_bmc_results_config()
print({"bmcEnabled": bmc.get("enabled"), "bmcUrl": bmc.get("url")})
result = perform_monitor_fetch(trigger="manual")
print({
  "status": result.get("status"),
  "governorFetch": result.get("governorFetch", {}).get("status"),
  "bmcFetch": result.get("bmcFetch", {}).get("status"),
  "sorkorSummaryKey": result.get("bmcFetch", {}).get("summaryKey"),
})
PY'
